---
title: "regression Project"
output: html_document
date: "2024-12-15"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
```{r}
data <- read.csv("alzheimers_disease_data.csv")
head(data)
```
```{r}
colnames(is.na(data))
```

```{r}
data <- subset(data, select = -c(PatientID, DoctorInCharge))
print("done")
```
```{r}
data_ <- subset(data, select = -Diagnosis)
data_scaled <- scale(data_)
head(data_scaled)
```
```{r}
pca_result <- prcomp(data_scaled, center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE)
summary(pca_result)
```

```{r}
eigenvalues <- pca_result$sdev^2
eigenvalues
```
```{r}
cum_var <- cumsum(eigenvalues) / sum(eigenvalues)
cum_var
```
```{r}
num_pcs <- which(cum_var >= 0.85)[1]
data_pca <- as.data.frame(pca_result$x)
data_reduced <- data_pca[, 1:num_pcs]
data_reduced <- cbind(data_reduced, Diagnosis = data$Diagnosis )
cat(dim(data_reduced))
head(data_reduced)
```


```{r}
library(caret)
library(e1071)
```

```{r}
# View the new dataset
head(data_reduced)
```
```{r}
train_index <- createDataPartition(data_reduced$Diagnosis, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data <- data_reduced[train_index, ]
test_data <- data_reduced[-train_index, ]

# Separate Features (X) and Target (Y) for each set
x_train <- train_data[, !names(train_data) %in% "Diagnosis"]
y_train <- as.factor(train_data$Diagnosis)

x_test <- test_data[, !names(test_data) %in% "Diagnosis"]
y_test <- as.factor(test_data$Diagnosis)

```
```{r}
cat("x_train : ", dim(x_train), "\n")
cat("y_train : ", length(y_train), "\n")
cat("x_test : ", dim(x_test), "\n")
cat("y_test : ", length(y_test), "\n")
```
```{r}
svm_model <- train(
  x = x_train,
  y = y_train,
  method = "svmRadial",
  preProcess = c("center", "scale"),
  trControl = trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5),
  probability = TRUE
)

summary(svm_model)
```

```{r}
svm_pred_train <- predict(svm_model, newdata = x_train)
confusionMatrix(svm_pred_train, y_train)
```
```{r}
pred <- predict(svm_model, x_test)
confusionMatrix(pred, y_test)
```
```{r}
#courbe Accuracy
str(svm_model$results)
# Si 'Accuracy' est disponible, tracer la courbe
ggplot(svm_model$results, aes(x = C, y = Accuracy)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  geom_point(color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Courbe d'évolution de l'Accuracy",
    x = "Paramètre C",
    y = "Accuracy"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
#courbe ROC
# Load necessary libraries
library(pROC)

# Assuming `y_test` contains true labels and `y_pred` contains predicted probabilities
roc_curve <- roc(as.numeric(y_test), as.numeric(pred))

# Extract FPR and TPR
fpr <- 1 - roc_curve$specificities
tpr <- roc_curve$sensitivities
plot(fpr, tpr, type = "l", col = "blue", lwd = 2,
     xlab = "False Positive Rate (FPR)", 
     ylab = "True Positive Rate (TPR)", 
     main = "ROC Curve")
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2, col = "red")


```
```{r}
#courbe DET
fpr <- 1 - roc_curve$specificities
fnr <- 1 - roc_curve$sensitivities

plot(fpr, fnr, type = "l", col = "blue", lwd = 2,
     xlab = "False Positive Rate (FPR) ", 
     ylab = "False Negative Rate (FNR) ", 
     main = "(DET) Curve"
     )  # Axes logarithmiques


```
